question: If ECR-221 changes the propulsion motors, what parts and documents are affected?
answer:
Engineering Change Request ECR-221. [1] # K-200 Motor Qualification Test Report — P-1041 / P-1042 [2] Bug KES-187. [3]

Related records are linked in the knowledge graph (40 relationship paths surfaced).

claims.text: # K-200 Motor Qualification Test Report — P-1041 / P-1042 [2]

citations.title: Replace propulsion motors P-1031 and ESCs P-1032 with P-1041/P-1042
citations.passage:
ECR-221 | Replace propulsion motors P-1031 and ESCs P-1032 with P-1041/P-1042

Engineering Change Request ECR-221.

Raised by: Marcus Thaine (Propulsion Lead), 2024-04-15.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2024-04-22.

Summary: Replace all four instances of the TorqueDrive 4012 490KV motors (P-1031) and ControlAir 60A ESCs (P-1032) with TorqueDrive 4014 420KV Enhanced motors (P-1041) and ControlAir 80A Enhanced ESCs (P-1042).

Rationale: Two thermal events during April 2024 testing demonstrated that P-1031 motors cannot meet the 40°C ambient temperature operating requirement:
- KES-187 (2024-04-02): Fwd-port P-1031 motor reached 108°C winding temperature during 45-min endurance run at 30°C ambient, triggering thermal cutout. Winding temperature rating is 85°C maximum continuous.
- KES-188 (2024-04-08): Fwd-stbd P-1031 motor ESC (P-1032) repeatedly overcurrented due to thermal throttling, causing motor sync loss.

Root cause (established at WIKI-031 Propulsion Thermal Review, 2024-04-10): The 4012 490KV winding design has insufficient stator-to-case thermal conductivity. The P-1031 motor was selected in 2023 without a hot-day endurance test.

The TorqueDrive 4014 420KV Enhanced (P-1041) uses an improved winding geometry that reduces stator temperature by approximately 18°C at the same operating point (per TorqueDrive data sheet DS-4014E rev 2). Paired with the ControlAir 80A Enhanced ESC (P-1042, copper heatsink) and P-1051 heatsink, the thermal margin is restored to 22°C at 40°C ambient.

citations.title: K-200 Motor Qualification Test Report — P-1041/P-1042
citations.passage:
DOC-445 | K-200 Motor Qualification Test Report — P-1041/P-1042

# K-200 Motor Qualification Test Report — P-1041 / P-1042

Document number: MD-DOC-445-A. Revision: A. Type: test-report. Author: Aisha Mbeki. Created: 2024-05-30. Last modified: 2024-06-10. Status: Released.

This report documents the qualification testing of the TorqueDrive 4014 420KV Enhanced motor (P-1041) and ControlAir 80A Enhanced ESC (P-1042) as replacements for P-1031 and P-1032. Change authority: ECN-318 / ECR-221. Background: thermal events KES-187 and KES-188, propulsion thermal review WIKI-031.

## §3 Thermal Test

Test configuration: P-1041 motor on P-1012 motor mount, P-1042 ESC with P-1051 heatsink, P-1044 motor controller board. Ambient temperature: 40°C (requirement maximum). Duration: 90 minutes at 75% throttle (representative endurance cruise).

Results:
- Motor winding temperature peak: 79°C (rated maximum: 85°C continuous). Margin: 6°C. PASS.
- ESC case temperature peak: 31°C (rated maximum: 70°C). Margin: 39°C. PASS.
- No thermal cutout events during 90-minute test. PASS.

Comparison with P-1031 at 40°C ambient (extrapolated from 30°C test data): estimated winding temperature would be 120°C — 35°C above rated maximum. FAIL expected.

## §4 Endurance Test

Duration: 3 x 90-minute flights at 40°C ambient. All flights completed without motor or ESC anomalies. Motor connector (P-1059) showed no heating. PASS.

## §5 Conclusion

P-1041 and P-1042 qualify for operational use per ECN-318 at ambient temperatures up to 40°C. P-1031 and P-1032 are obsolete and shall not be used in operational aircraft.

citations.title: [Bug] Motor P-1031 fwd-port thermal cutout during 45-minute endurance run
citations.passage:
KES-187 | [Bug] Motor P-1031 fwd-port thermal cutout during 45-minute endurance run

Bug KES-187. Reporter: Aisha Mbeki. Assignee: Marcus Thaine (Propulsion Lead). Status: Done. Priority: Critical. Created: 2024-04-02. Resolved: 2024-04-22.

Description: During a 45-minute endurance run on 2 April 2024 at ambient temperature 30°C, the fwd-port TorqueDrive 4012 490KV motor (P-1031) triggered its internal thermal cutout at T+38 minutes. Motor controller board (P-1044) thermal logging showed winding temperature reached 108°C, against a rated maximum continuous temperature of 85°C. The motor shutdown caused an autopilot attitude disturbance event but the FCU (P-1091) recovered within 2 seconds.

The ESC (P-1032) data showed no overcurrent prior to the motor cutout, confirming the thermal event originated in the motor windings rather than the ESC.

This is the first thermal event on the propulsion system. Ambient temperature was 30°C; the customer requirement specifies 40°C maximum operating temperature.

Acceptance criteria: Motor replacement ECR-221 approved and ECN-318 issued.

Linked to: KES-188 (related ESC overcurrent event), ECR-221 (motor replacement ECR), WIKI-031 (propulsion thermal review meeting).

sources.title: Replace propulsion motors P-1031 and ESCs P-1032 with P-1041/P-1042
sources.body:
ECR-221 | Replace propulsion motors P-1031 and ESCs P-1032 with P-1041/P-1042

Engineering Change Request ECR-221.

Raised by: Marcus Thaine (Propulsion Lead), 2024-04-15.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2024-04-22.

Summary: Replace all four instances of the TorqueDrive 4012 490KV motors (P-1031) and ControlAir 60A ESCs (P-1032) with TorqueDrive 4014 420KV Enhanced motors (P-1041) and ControlAir 80A Enhanced ESCs (P-1042).

Rationale: Two thermal events during April 2024 testing demonstrated that P-1031 motors cannot meet the 40°C ambient temperature operating requirement:
- KES-187 (2024-04-02): Fwd-port P-1031 motor reached 108°C winding temperature during 45-min endurance run at 30°C ambient, triggering thermal cutout. Winding temperature rating is 85°C maximum continuous.
- KES-188 (2024-04-08): Fwd-stbd P-1031 motor ESC (P-1032) repeatedly overcurrented due to thermal throttling, causing motor sync loss.

Root cause (established at WIKI-031 Propulsion Thermal Review, 2024-04-10): The 4012 490KV winding design has insufficient stator-to-case thermal conductivity. The P-1031 motor was selected in 2023 without a hot-day endurance test.

The TorqueDrive 4014 420KV Enhanced (P-1041) uses an improved winding geometry that reduces stator temperature by approximately 18°C at the same operating point (per TorqueDrive data sheet DS-4014E rev 2). Paired with the ControlAir 80A Enhanced ESC (P-1042, copper heatsink) and P-1051 heatsink, the thermal margin is restored to 22°C at 40°C ambient.

sources.title: [Bug] Motor P-1031 fwd-port thermal cutout during 45-minute endurance run
sources.body:
KES-187 | [Bug] Motor P-1031 fwd-port thermal cutout during 45-minute endurance run

Bug KES-187. Reporter: Aisha Mbeki. Assignee: Marcus Thaine (Propulsion Lead). Status: Done. Priority: Critical. Created: 2024-04-02. Resolved: 2024-04-22.

Description: During a 45-minute endurance run on 2 April 2024 at ambient temperature 30°C, the fwd-port TorqueDrive 4012 490KV motor (P-1031) triggered its internal thermal cutout at T+38 minutes. Motor controller board (P-1044) thermal logging showed winding temperature reached 108°C, against a rated maximum continuous temperature of 85°C. The motor shutdown caused an autopilot attitude disturbance event but the FCU (P-1091) recovered within 2 seconds.

The ESC (P-1032) data showed no overcurrent prior to the motor cutout, confirming the thermal event originated in the motor windings rather than the ESC.

This is the first thermal event on the propulsion system. Ambient temperature was 30°C; the customer requirement specifies 40°C maximum operating temperature.

Acceptance criteria: Motor replacement ECR-221 approved and ECN-318 issued.

Linked to: KES-188 (related ESC overcurrent event), ECR-221 (motor replacement ECR), WIKI-031 (propulsion thermal review meeting).

sources.title: GCS software architecture v2: archive SRC-001 gcs_main.py, introduce SRC-024 backend service
sources.body:
ECR-225 | GCS software architecture v2: archive SRC-001 gcs_main.py, introduce SRC-024 backend service

Engineering Change Request ECR-225.

Raised by: Jordan Kim (GCS Software Engineer), 2024-10-22.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Felix Herrera (Systems Integration), 2024-10-30.

Summary: Refactor the GCS software from a monolithic architecture (SRC-001 gcs_main.py) to a split backend/frontend architecture (SRC-024 gcs_ui_backend.py + browser-based frontend).

Rationale: Three converging problems identified (KES-173 epic, KES-175 story):
1. Monolith scale: SRC-001 gcs_main.py grew to 8,400 lines. Telemetry decoding, command encoding, map rendering, and mission planning are in a single synchronous event loop — unmaintainable.
2. Encryption integration blocker: Integration of the AES-256 encryption stack (KES-201, P-1127 FPGA module via SRC-018) revealed that synchronous command dispatch in v1 blocks the entire UI thread during encryption operations (800ms–2s UI freezes). The encryption latency is unavoidable with hardware AES.
3. Protocol agility: The v1 architecture couples the MAVLink protocol implementation directly to the UI layer, making protocol updates (required by DOC-471 Comms ICD Rev C) extremely difficult.

The v2 architecture agreed at WIKI-058 (GCS Architecture Review, 2024-10-15) uses asyncio Python backend (SRC-024) with a WebSocket API to a Vue.js frontend. The backend owns all drone communications via P-1167 (GCS Radio Unit). DOC-479 (GCS Software Architecture Document) to be created.

Impact:
- SRC-001 (gcs_main.py): Archived (kept for reference)
- SRC-002 (gcs_telemetry.py): Refactored into SRC-024 backend
- SRC-024 (gcs_ui_backend.py): New, asyncio backend service
- SRC-025 (gcs_map_layer.py): Replaced by JavaScript map component
- DOC-479: New GCS Software Architecture Document
- SRC-017 (mission_planner.py): Adapted to SRC-024 API

sources.title: [Epic] Comms datalink encryption upgrade — AES-256-GCM
sources.body:
KES-201 | [Epic] Comms datalink encryption upgrade — AES-256-GCM

Epic KES-201. Reporter: Priya Nair (Comms/Datalink Engineer). Assignee: Priya Nair. Status: Done. Priority: High. Created: 2025-01-10. Resolved: 2025-03-01.

Description: End-to-end AES-256-GCM encryption for the K-200 command and telemetry datalink, required by customer contract security addendum (ref KES-203) by milestone M4 (March 2025).

Background: December 2024 penetration test (MD-SEC-2024-11) found the unencrypted FreeWave P-1125 datalink vulnerable to command injection. This epic covers the hardware (P-1127 FPGA encryption module), the radio upgrade (P-1125 → P-1126 for bandwidth headroom), the firmware implementation (SRC-018), the GCS protocol stack update (SRC-024), and the ICD updates (DOC-471, DOC-472).

Design review: WIKI-071 (Comms Encryption Upgrade Review, 2025-01-22).
ECR: ECR-229. ECN: ECN-322 (approved 2025-02-05).

Sub-stories: KES-203 (AES-256 implementation), KES-203 contract requirement story.

sources.title: Programme Status Gate 2 — 2024-11-28
sources.body:
WIKI-055 | Programme Status Gate 2 — 2024-11-28

# Programme Status Gate 2 — 2024-11-28

**Attendees:** Marcus Thaine, Dev Patel, Aisha Mbeki, Rowan Ellery

## Summary

This page covers the EMC mitigation strategy for the Propulsion subsystem. Key items discussed and actions agreed are recorded here.

## Actions

- Follow-up with Sam Dreher on outstanding items.
